summary: Clean soundboard capture of a stellar May ’77 performance, highlighted by a perfect first set and a standout Eyes of the World→Not Fade Away sequence.
review: The show is praised as a hidden gem from May 1977 with an exceptional first set that many consider tour‑best versions and a second set that, while mixed, delivers adventurous moments like the Eyes of the World > Not Fade Away jam. The Serafin soundboard source is regarded as the cleanest available despite some patched edits, making the recording enjoyable throughout.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers rate the show highly (4‑5 stars) and emphasize strong musical performance; recording is described as clean and accurate, yielding a combined high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Serafin_11614 SBD is the cleanest version; minor edits include patched Estimated Prophet, fade‑out/in between Eyes>NFA, and a short missing segment in Around. Overall clarity and balance are praised.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set flows flawlessly and is considered a must‑have; second set is average overall but contains adventurous peaks (Eyes>Not Fade Away) and a few weaker moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Long, exploratory jams on Sugaree and rhythmic experiments on Big River are highlighted; his guitar work praised throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocal/guitar performance on Jack-a-Roe described as "simply incredible."
